depression.description: I often feel like I'm stuck in a cycle of conflict with my partner's family, especially with Candace. It reminds me of my relationship with my father, and I just don't know how to break free from this feeling of powerlessness.
I can't shake the feeling that I'm unlovable and that my worth is tied to how others treat me. When Candace is rude, it reinforces that belief, and I feel even more isolated.
Every time I think about the upcoming family vacation, I feel a wave of anxiety. I dread the thought of interacting with Candace and worry that I'll just end up feeling worse.
I'm terrified of standing up for myself because I believe it will only lead to more drama and conflict within the family. This fear keeps me from asserting my needs.
I find myself going over past conversations with Candace in my head, replaying them and feeling sad about how I was treated. It's hard to let go of those feelings.

Delusion.topic: Belief in family loyalty over self-care
Delusion.description: I feel like I have to prioritize family harmony over my own well-being, even if it means accepting poor treatment. It's like I'm conditioned to believe that I must endure this.

Delusion.topic: Fear of abandonment if I assert myself
Delusion.description: I worry that if I speak up or set boundaries, I'll be abandoned or cause a rift in the family. This fear keeps me silent even when I'm hurting.
